senses.definition: (of food or drink) to be consumed by someone

examples.x: The rich aroma of freshly baked bread made it impossible to resist having a slice pass my lips.
She tried to maintain a healthy diet, ensuring that no sugary drinks or junk food would pass her lips.
As a food critic, he had tasted countless dishes that had passed his lips, but this one was truly exceptional.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv wˈʌnz nˈoʊz ɪn ɐ bˈʊk

senses.definition: to be fully concentrated on the book one is reading, particularly with a lot of interest or enthusiasm

examples.x: Julie had her nose in a book during the whole journey.
Even during family gatherings, he couldn't resist having his nose in a book, engrossed in his latest novel.
Despite the noise in the coffee shop, he had his nose in a book, enjoying his favorite detective series.

senses.definition: used to refer to a situation in which everything is progressing in a way it should without anything unusual or unexpected happening

examples.x: Despite the unexpected power outage, the company resumed business as usual once the electricity was restored.
The staff carried on with business as usual even during the busiest time of the year, showing remarkable dedication.
In times of crisis, it's essential for a leader to reassure the team that it's business as usual, maintaining stability.
After the merger, the company aimed to integrate the two organizations smoothly and continue with business as usual.
Even with the change in management, the goal was to ensure that day-to-day operations remained business as usual for the employees.

senses.definition: used to refer to the act of doing something without thinking about it because one has been doing it for a long time and it has become a habit
used to refer to a person who is engaging in the same dishonest behavior as they previously demonstrated

examples.x: I knew he was up to his old tricks when I found my keys missing again.
She's always up to her tricks when it comes to organizing surprise parties for her friends.
The cat was up to its old tricks, sneaking into the pantry and knocking over the cereal boxes.
When the children are quiet, it's a sign that they're up to their tricks, so I need to check on them.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who always does the same things in the exact same way they did before

examples.x: Despite the new gym opening nearby, John remained a creature of habit, continuing to exercise at his usual time and place.
Sarah's coworkers affectionately teased her for being a creature of habit, as she always ordered the same coffee and pastry from the café each morning.
Even during vacation, Mark couldn't shake his creature of habit tendencies, waking up at the same time each day and going for his morning run.
Grandma was a creature of habit when it came to mealtime, insisting on having dinner promptly at 6 p.m. every evening.
Jenny's friends encouraged her to break out of her routine and try something new, but she remained a creature of habit, sticking to her familiar schedule and activities.

senses.definition: (of a particular word or topic) to be said by someone

examples.x: He was so loyal to his friends that he vowed never to let a word of their secret plan pass his lips.
The wise old man rarely let a harsh word pass his lips, always choosing his words carefully.
She had a strict policy of never allowing gossip to pass her lips, believing in the importance of discretion.
In the courtroom, the witness hesitated before letting the truth about the incident finally pass her lips.
When it comes to sharing important news, I trust her implicitly, knowing that nothing I say will pass her lips.

senses.definition: a person who spends a lot of time working out or exercising at the gym

examples.x: She transformed from a couch potato into a dedicated gym rat, embracing a healthier lifestyle.
He's a true gym rat, hitting the gym every day without fail.
Even on weekends, he's at the gym; he's a real gym rat.
The gym rat's dedication paid off as he achieved his fitness goals in record time.
She's become a gym rat not just for the physical benefits but also for the mental clarity it provides.

senses.definition: used when describing one's mundane life, in which nothing new or exciting happens
a hasty attempt at cleaning or tidying something that does not effectively solve the problem

examples.x: He did a cat's lick of the car, just enough to make it look presentable for the trip, but it needed a thorough wash.
She gave the kitchen a quick cat's lick before guests arrived, but it still needed a proper cleaning.
The housecleaner did a cat's lick of the living room, dusting only the visible surfaces and neglecting the corners.
